extension.valueMarkdown:
In Georgia, Harold established with a new PCP and a geriatric neurologist at Emory. His MMSE dropped from 22/30 (mild) to 17/30 (moderate) over 6 months. He was switched from donepezil to a donepezil-memantine combination. His diabetes and hypertension management continued with his new PCP. He had one ED visit for a mechanical fall (no fracture, CT head negative).

Margaret, as his authorized representative, uses a patient-facing app to access Harold's records across both the Florida and Georgia systems.

Four sites across two states (FL, GA). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on jurisdictions (FL vs GA), organizations (4 providers), periods (pre-move Florida records vs post-move Georgia records), and scopes (can filter to just cognitive assessments or just diabetes management).

code.text: Alzheimer's disease, mild to moderate stage

note.text: PHQ-2 improved to 2/6 at March 2025 visit, below positive screen threshold. Previously 3/6 at November 2024 new patient visit. Social work referral for bereavement support previously placed. Continue to monitor.

Mechanical fall in February 2025 — nocturnal fall from toilet. ED workup negative for acute injury beyond forehead laceration and wrist contusion, both now healed. Reassessed at this visit: no orthostatic hypotension on testing, TUG 14 seconds (mildly increased, acceptable range), no sedating medications, home modifications completed. Low risk for recurrence.
Weight 186 lbs, down from 190 lbs five months ago and 198 lbs over past decade. BMI 26.7. Monitor nutritional status in setting of Alzheimer's disease. Recommended supplemental nutrition shakes.

code.text: Referral to social work for bereavement support and caregiver resources

extension.valueMarkdown: Harold Washington, age 78, presented as a new patient to Dr. Anita Desai at DeKalb Primary Care after relocating from Jacksonville, Florida to live with his daughter Margaret Washington-Clarke in Decatur, Georgia.
Harold presented for fasting blood draw as ordered at the November 14 new patient visit. No vitals taken.

Dr. Desai's office called Margaret to check on Harold five days after his emergency department visit on February 8 (mechanical fall, forehead laceration repaired with dermabond, CT head and right hip X-ray negative).

reasonCode.text: Follow-up, chronic disease management, fall prevention
